page: Microsoft Azure E128ads_v7 is an Eadsv7-series virtual machine configured with 128 dedicated vCPUs. Powered by an AMD processor on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 1024.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

The server includes 7559 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses. There are no integrated graphics processing units, as the GPU count is zero.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local storage capacity position this instance as a resource-dense option for large-scale workloads. The hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive applications, large databases, enterprise applications, and data warehousing tasks that require significant local storage and memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure E128ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine from the Eadsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD processor, paired with 1024.0 GB of system memory, establishing an 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance is equipped with 7559 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage options, though it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses or dedicated GPU hardware. This high resource density makes the server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive enterprise deployments. The hardware configuration is optimized for large-scale databases, memory-heavy analytical workloads, and data warehousing applications requiring substantial local storage capacity.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 1024 GB of RAM, and 7559 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring one hundred and twenty-eight dedicated cores and over seven terabytes of local storage.
